get.description: Returns a list of participants for an event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The ID of the event.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: A filter for results that match the specified name. For example, name=Smith returns participants with a name containing Smith.

parameters.name: participation_level
parameters.description: A filter for results that match the specified level of participant involvement in the event. For example, participation_level=level returns participants with a participation level of level.

parameters.name: attended_filter
parameters.description: A filter to include or exclude participants marked as attended.

parameters.name: is_constituent_filter
parameters.description: A filter to include or exclude participants who are constituents.

parameters.name: email_eligible_filter
parameters.description: A filter to include or exclude participants based on whether they have a primary email address that's not marked Do not email.

parameters.name: phone_call_eligible_filter
parameters.description: A filter to include or exclude participants based on whether they have a primary phone number that's not marked Do not call.

parameters.name: invitation_status
parameters.description:
A filter for results that match the specified invitation status. For example, invitation_status=NotInvited returns participants with a status of Not Invited.

Available values:
  • NotApplicable
  • NotInvited
  • Invited

items.description:
The possible states for a constituent's invitation to an event.

Available values:

  • NotApplicable - Not applicable.
  • NotInvited - Not invited.
  • Invited - Invited.

parameters.name: rsvp_status
parameters.description:
A filter for results that match the specified RSVP status. For example, rsvp_status=Attending,NotApplicable returns participants with a status of Attending or Not Applicable.

Available values:
  • NoResponse
  • Attending
  • Declined
  • Interested
  • Canceled
  • Waitlisted
  • NotApplicable

items.description:
The possible states for a participant's RSVP for an event.

Available values:

  • NoResponse - No response.
  • Attending - Attending.
  • Declined - Declined.
  • Interested - Interested.
  • Canceled - Canceled.
  • Waitlisted - Waitlisted.
  • NotApplicable - Not applicable.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for results created on or after the specified date. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for results modified on or after the specified date. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

parameters.name: fees_paid_filter
parameters.description: A filter to include or exclude participants that have paid or not paid their fees.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Represents a list of fields to include on the returned records. Supported request fields include: id, constituent_id, name, phone, email, participation_level, attended, rsvp_status, invitation_status, rsvp_date, total_fees, total_paid, host, guests, name_tag, seat, seating_notes, seating_group, memberships, lookup_id, is_constituent, summary_note, date_added, date_modified, donations, revenue, participant_options, registration_form, online_data_health, fees. See the sample response output for all the return fields.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Represents a list of fields to sort the results by. Returns a list that sorts participants based on the supplied fields. Results are in ascending order by default, and a '-' sign denotes descending order. For example, sort=invitation_status,-name sorts participants by the invitation_status field in ascending order and then by the name field in descending order. If no fields are provided, results are sorted by participant last name in ascending order.

parameters.name: participant_option_id
parameters.description: A filter for participants who are assigned to a specific participant option.

parameters.name: participant_option_values
parameters.description: A filter for results that match values for a specific participant option. For example, participant_option_values=true returns participants who selected true if you also include the participant_option_id for a boolean type option in the request. If you include a participant_option_id for a list type option, include the ID for the value to return. For example, participant_option_values=1476f7ea-816e-4a97-9f33-b99a5acb26cd returns participants who selected the value associated with id 1476f7ea-816e-4a97-9f33-b99a5acb26cd.

parameters.name: registration_form_include_type
parameters.description:
A filter for participants who are registered through online registration forms. For example, registration_form_include_type=Specific returns participants who registered for forms you indicate in the registration_form_ids filter.

Available values:

  • Any - Filters for participants from any online registration form.
  • Specific - Filters for participants from specific online registration forms.
  • None - Filters for participants that were not created from an online registration form.

parameters.name: registration_form_ids
parameters.description: A filter for participants who registered through specific online registration forms.

parameters.name: online_data_health
parameters.description:
A filter for results that match the specified online data health statuses. For example, online_data_health=PossibleDuplicate,Matched returns participants with a status of Possible Duplicate or Matched.

Available values:
  • Matched
  • PossibleDuplicate
  • NewNamedGuest
  • NewConstituent
  • FormerPossibleDuplicate
  • ManuallyChanged

items.description:
The possible states for a participant's online data health status.

Available values:

  • Matched - Indicates that a constituent from an online registration was matched to an existing constituent record.
  • PossibleDuplicate - Indicates that a new constituent was created from online registrations because there were records that matched on name but not on contact information.
  • NewNamedGuest - Indicates that a named guest record was created because the constituent was already registered as a participant, or there was not enough contact information from online registrations to create a constituent record.
  • NewConstituent - Indicates the constituent record was created because the system didn't find existing records that matched constituent information from online registrations.
  • FormerPossibleDuplicate - Indicates the possible duplicate has been resolved or the system determined there are no more possible duplicates.
  • ManuallyChanged - Indicates the constituent from online registrations that was linked to the participant was manually changed by a user.

parameters.name: event_fee_include_type
parameters.description:
A filter for participants who have event fees. For example, event_fee_include_type_filter=Specific returns participants who have event fees you indicate in the event_fee_ids_filter filter.

Available values:

  • Any - Filters for participants with any fee
  • Specific - Filters for participants with specific fees.
  • None - Filters for participants with no fees.

parameters.name: event_fee_ids
parameters.description: A filter for participants who have specific event fees.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 10,000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection and count of filtered participants.

post.description:
Creates a new participant for an event.

Note: Host participants and standalone participants have the Host registrant type. To create a participant with the Guest registrant type, include a host_id.
  1. To create a Host participant, pass a constituent_id (must be a constituent record) and do not pass a host_id.
  2. To create a Guest participant that will be linked to an existing record, pass a constituent_id (constituent or non-constituent record) and pass the participant ID of the Host as the host_id.
  3. To create a Guest participant that will create a new non-constituent record, do not pass a constituent_id (a new unnamed guest will be created as a non-constituent) and pass the participant ID of the Host as the host_id.

online_data_health.description:
For a participant who registers online, shows their online data health status, such as if they were matched to an existing constituent or added as a possible duplicate.

Available values:

  • Matched - Indicates that a constituent from an online registration was matched to an existing constituent record.
  • PossibleDuplicate - Indicates that a new constituent was created from online registrations because there were records that matched on name but not on contact information.
  • NewNamedGuest - Indicates that a named guest record was created because the constituent was already registered as a participant, or there was not enough contact information from online registrations to create a constituent record.
  • NewConstituent - Indicates the constituent record was created because the system didn't find existing records that matched constituent information from online registrations.
  • FormerPossibleDuplicate - Indicates the possible duplicate has been resolved or the system determined there are no more possible duplicates.
  • ManuallyChanged - Indicates the constituent from online registrations that was linked to the participant was manually changed by a user.
